results.possibleIdentity: S. Fascher was an electronic engineer and researcher active in the late 1960s, primarily associated with the New York University (NYU) Research Division. Based in Yonkers, New York, Fascher specialized in medical electronics and biomedical engineering. In 1968, they co-authored a significant technical paper titled "A transcutaneous power transformer," published in the Transactions of the American Society for Artificial Internal Organs (ASAIO). This work, conducted alongside notable NYU faculty and engineers including Dr. George H. Myers and Alfred Thumin, focused on developing inductive power systems to energize artificial hearts through intact skin. Fascher was an engineer member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that fostered collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. This association was shared with their NYU colleague Alfred Thumin, who was a prominent figure in E.A.T.'s technical projects. Fascher's involvement in the late 1960s coincides with the peak of the artist-engineer movement in New York.
